title: 重命名核心代码符号与服务文案
summary: Rebranded the active Ralph runtime surface to Harness across exported helpers/types, user-facing language-pack copy, help titles, runtime/status messages, prompt context, synthesized artifact text, and associated test expectations.
architectureNotes: The story changed several core execution surfaces, but kept persistence paths stable by retaining .harness-runner.
Tests were updated together with exported symbol renames to keep the module graph and assertions coherent.
riskReasons: The story changes Harness core execution or packaging surfaces.
Icon and packaged VSIX filenames still carry legacy ralph branding outside the active runtime copy.
releaseNotes: Core extension APIs and user-facing copy now use Harness instead of Ralph.
Status bar, help pages, prompt contracts, synthesized artifact wording, and test expectations were aligned to the new name.
The .harness-runner artifact root and legacy ralph-runner settings cleanup behavior were preserved.
rollbackHints: Rollback should revert the runtime/help/localization/test rename together rather than selectively undoing only one module.
Use the story-scoped commit as the primary rollback unit before considering partial reversions.
followUps: Rename or replace icon/package assets if visual branding parity is required.
Audit repository metadata and packaged outputs for remaining legacy ralph naming.

dimensions.label: Architecture Consistency
dimensions.summary: Core rename surfaces and tests are aligned.

dimensions.label: Acceptance Coverage
dimensions.summary: Acceptance rename targets are reflected in code and strings.

dimensions.label: Change Scope Control
dimensions.summary: The story did not alter prd.json or runtime artifact-root paths.

dimensions.label: Verifiability
dimensions.summary: Compile and test-compilation evidence are attached.
